seoTitle: How to Export Your Google Workspace Data and Back It Up
metaDescription: Back up or export Google Workspace data using Google Takeout or the Admin Console, covering Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and shared drive files for every user.
intro: Whether you're switching accounts, backing up company data, or preparing to offboard a user, exporting Google Workspace data is something every admin eventually needs to do. This guide covers exporting individual user data with Google Takeout as well as full admin-level exports for every user on the domain. It also touches on choosing export formats and destinations, and best practices to follow before offboarding someone.

steps.heading: Export user data with Google Takeout
steps.body: Use Google Takeout to download an individual user's Drive files, Gmail, Calendar, and other Workspace data.

steps.heading: Run an admin-level export for all users
steps.body: For organization-wide backups, use the Admin Console to export data across all users rather than one account at a time.

steps.heading: Follow best practices before offboarding
steps.body: Complete a full export before deleting or offboarding a user so no company data is lost in the process.

steps.heading: Choose your export format and destination
steps.body: Pick where the exported data should go, such as Drive or Dropbox, and select the format that best suits how you plan to use it.

faq.question: Can I export just one user's Gmail and Drive data?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Takeout lets you export an individual user's Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and other Workspace data.

faq.question: How do I back up data for every user in my organization?
faq.answer: The Admin Console offers an admin-level export option that covers all users rather than exporting one account at a time.

faq.question: Should I export a user's data before offboarding them?
faq.answer: Yes, running a full export before deleting or offboarding a user is recommended so their data isn't lost.

faq.question: Can I export shared drive files as well as personal ones?
faq.answer: Yes, shared drive data can be downloaded securely alongside individual user files.
